downstream_consumers.agents/eval/metrics.py: read-only reuse (score_arm, ArmScore, QueryScore) -- not modified, no risk to its existing passing tests
downstream_consumers.agents/eval/baselines/graphrag_lite.py: read-only reuse (EntityGraph, retrieve_documents, DEFAULT_MAX_HOPS) -- not modified, no risk to its existing passing tests

risks.risk: Duplicating recall/precision or graph-traversal logic instead of reusing eval.metrics / eval.baselines.graphrag_lite
risks.mitigation: New module imports both directly; no reimplementation.

risks.risk: Test suite accidentally making a live network/Ollama call, violating offline-only scope constraint
risks.mitigation: New test file uses a deterministic in-file _StubLLMClient (no network), mirroring test_graphrag_baseline.py's established stub pattern; no OllamaClient import in the new test file.

risks.risk: Over-scoping into real corpus-growth-checkpoint wiring (subtask 5.3.4 territory, gated to live benchmark run)
risks.mitigation: Checkpoint concept is modeled generically (an arbitrary label + arbitrary EntityGraph/doc set) so the comparison primitive is checkpoint-agnostic; no real corpus/datasets.py wiring is added, matching 5.2.3's own disclosed fixture-only scope boundary.

backward_compatibility: No existing file is modified; purely additive. Zero risk to already-merged/passing tests.
test_impact: New pytest file agents/eval/test_traversal_precision_check.py only; no changes to existing test files.
